summary: Large fighter-oriented cleanup pass that imported equivalent commits from PR #636 up to 3cdac6c16058487654f4e9359b926bc1a9b1bbd5. The PR mostly improved names, headers, typed data structures, and formatting across fighter, baselib, stage, item, menu, and assembly files. Notable semantic cleanup included renaming func_803901F0 to GObj_Create, Fighter_80068E98 to Fighter_Create, func_8007E620 to Fighter_SetAccessory, lbl_80458FD0 to fighter_alloc_data, and lbl_804D6544 to ftPartsTable; adding ftPart, FighterPartsTable, and DObjList; replacing raw fighter part indices such as 51 with ThrowN; and moving HSD_ForeachAnim to a more specific aobj header with a typed signature. Much of the 80-file diff is mechanical whitespace/newline normalization in assembly, supported by adding tools/newlines.sh to make format.
what_changed: Added -msgstyle gcc to CFLAGS in the Makefile.
Added tools/newlines.sh to the format target and added a shebang to the script.
Normalized trailing whitespace/newlines across many assembly files, visible in repeated changes such as `blr ` to `blr` and `bctr ` to `bctr`.
Renamed baselib GObj constructor func_803901F0 to GObj_Create in include/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h, src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjplink.c, C sources, and many assembly callsites.
Renamed Fighter_80068E98 to Fighter_Create and its helper Fighter_80068E98_Inline2 to Fighter_Create_Inline2.
Renamed the fighter object allocator global lbl_80458FD0 to fighter_alloc_data.
Renamed lbl_804D6544 to ftPartsTable and typed it as FighterPartsTable**.
Added ftPart enum entries for fighter model parts, including ThrowN, and used ThrowN instead of a raw index in Samus grapple beam code.
Added FighterPartsTable for PlCo.dat joint/part mapping and DObjList for the fighter DObj array/count pair.
Changed Fighter.x5F0 raw HSD_DObj** field into x5EC_dobj_list and updated users to access x5EC_dobj_list.data.
Renamed ftSamus_801287C4 to ftSamus_CreateThrowGrappleBeam and improved parameter/local names such as action_state, anim_speed, and beam.
Replaced magic HSD_ForeachAnim arguments in Samus code with named constants JOBJ_TYPE, ALL_TYPE_MASK filtering, and AOBJ_ARG_AF.
Moved HSD_ForeachAnim declaration from include/functions.h into include/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.h with a typed varargs signature.
Fixed ALL_TYPE_MASK in object.h from MASK_OF(HSD_MAX_TYPE) to MASK_OF(HSD_MAX_TYPE) - 1.
Changed HSD_JObjDesc references in pobj.h and robj.h to HSD_Joint where the descriptors point at runtime joint data.
Replaced several inline assembly `#pragma peephole on` endings with `#pragma push` / `#pragma pop` wrappers in files such as gmmain_lib.c and ground.c.
smart_moves: Renamed globally-used unknown symbols in both C and assembly, keeping callsites consistent while improving searchability.
Introduced small named structs and enums before replacing field uses, making later fighter decomp work less dependent on raw offsets and magic indices.
Converted a raw DObj pointer field into DObjList without hiding the underlying layout, preserving matching-relevant offset clarity while documenting count/data intent.
Used domain names from asset data such as PlCo.dat and fighter parts to anchor types in observed file formats rather than speculative gameplay names.
Moved a broad prototype out of include/functions.h into the relevant baselib aobj header, reducing global header clutter and making ownership clearer.
Automated newline cleanup through the format target instead of relying on one-off manual whitespace edits.
Accepted review feedback to use `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, making the formatting helper executable through the Makefile on more systems.
decomp_lessons: When a cleanup pass renames a function used by many undecompiled files, update assembly call labels too so symbol names stay coherent across C and asm slices.
Prefer named enums for skeleton/model part indices; replacing values like 51 with ThrowN makes future matching code clearer without changing generated code intent.
For asset-backed globals, document the source asset near the type, as done with FighterPartsTable being marked as a table in PlCo.dat.
A raw pointer field may actually be a small inline struct; DObjList showed that an apparent filler plus pointer around x5EC/x5F0 could be represented as count plus data.
Move variadic or broad prototypes to their subsystem header once their ownership is known; this improves include hygiene and type checking.
Whitespace-only assembly churn can be large and noisy, so future reviewers should separate mechanical formatting from semantic decomp cleanup where possible.
For inline asm wrappers, use balanced `#pragma push` / `#pragma pop` rather than leaving compiler state changed with one-way pragmas like `#pragma peephole on`.
naming_conventions: Use descriptive subsystem-prefixed names for known fighter APIs: Fighter_Create, Fighter_SetAccessory.
Use baselib object naming for constructors: GObj_Create instead of address-like func_803901F0.
Use lower_snake_case for known global allocation data: fighter_alloc_data.
Use ft-prefixed fighter asset globals/types where appropriate: ftPartsTable, ftPart.
Use exact model-part names for ftPart enum values, preserving asset-style names such as TopN, TransN, ThrowN, and TransN2.
Use field names that expose structure contents and offset: x5EC_dobj_list.data instead of x5F0.
When renaming character-specific routines, include character and action purpose: ftSamus_CreateThrowGrappleBeam.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mass-updated assembly references from func_803901F0 to GObj_Create so asm remains link-compatible with the renamed C symbol.
Mass-updated assembly references to ftPartsTable and Fighter_SetAccessory after C-side symbol renames.
Normalized generated assembly comments by removing trailing spaces after instructions such as blr, bctr, and blrl.
Added -msgstyle gcc to compiler flags, likely to make compiler diagnostics easier for build tooling and contributors; the PR text does not explain deeper matching impact.
Wrapped inline assembly blocks with `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop` to restore compiler pragma state after asm islands.
Kept comments noting matching constraints, such as the warning that casting through `(void*)` in Fighter_Create_Inline2 would stop Fighter_Create from matching.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether invoking tools/newlines.sh directly from the format target would work, noting uncertainty about make behavior.
Pointed out that the script needs to be run with `sh tools/newlines.sh` or have a shebang.
Requested `#!/usr/bin/env bash` for better distro compatibility; the final diff shows that shebang in tools/newlines.sh.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that build on ftPartsTable or FighterPartsTable to identify the full PlCo.dat part mapping.
Search later cleanup passes for remaining raw fighter part indices that should use ftPart enum names.
Search for remaining func_803901F0 references after PR #638 to confirm GObj_Create migration coverage.
Search for remaining x5F0 or raw DObj array uses that should become x5EC_dobj_list.data.
Look for later changes to ALL_TYPE_MASK to confirm whether MASK_OF(HSD_MAX_TYPE) - 1 remained the accepted fix.
Search for additional `#pragma peephole on` instances that may need push/pop cleanup.
